title: WarmStart LAMP®
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"emgq4","text":"How to run nucleic acid amplification using the Warm-Start LAMP kit.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d7vrf","text":"Each reaction produces 25 μL.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dkroq","text":"For the original protocol, look at:  .","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":36,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"3rft4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8k34p","text":"*I have not verified the details of this protocol, I would like to remove it, but I cannot since it has been forked.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5n84g","text":"If you would like to use this protocol, I would recommend using the PDF above instead.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fciq2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"file","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"E361D5C9342911EEAFD10A58A9FEAC02","original_name":"LAMP Protocol.pdf","placeholder":"https://www.protocols.io/img/extensions/pdf.png","source":"https://content.protocols.io/bcnva.pdf"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bou5h","text":"Gloves must be worn at all times.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6in2i","text":"Use all precautions to avoid contamination when making reaction mixture.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7i0of","text":"Always pipette mix each reagent in aliquot before pipetting.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cqdeh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

100 rxns","mol_weight":0,"sku":"E1700S","url":"https://www.neb.com/E1700","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":2,"name":"New England Biolabs","link":"https://www.neb.com/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/2.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/2.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":119310,"guid":"419821DC341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"DNAse/RNAse free distilled water","mol_weight":0,"sku":"10977023","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":95,"name":"Thermo Fisher Scientific","link":"https://www.thermofisher.com/us/en/home.html","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Spray entire work area with 70% EtOH including pipettes, tip holder used for holding PCR tubes, and work surface. Wipe with a paper towel.
Spray entire work area with RNAway.
Take styrofoam container to Marley 527 (directly across from Marley 509) and fill halfway with ice.
Set PCR tube holder on ice, and allow to cool for 00:03:00 .
Transfer Master Mix, primers, RNAse free water, and target tubes from freezer to PCR tube holder on ice.
Allow reagents to thaw on ice

Carefully obtain (2) 0.2 mL PCR tubes. Label one with "NTC" and the other "TARG". These will be your reaction vessels. To avoid contamination when grabbing PCR tubes, only touch the outside of tubes. Avoid touching the inside of the caps of other tubes in this process. This is critical.
Using a 2-20 μL pipette, transfer 9 µL RNAse free water into the TARGET reaction vessel. Using a 2-20 μL pipette, transfe 10 µL RNAse free water into the NTC reaction vessel.
Using a 0.5-10 μL pipette, transfer 2.5 µL Primer to each reaction vessel. Primer will depend on what your target is. For E. Coli OH157, the primer tube is labelled with an 'F'.
Using a 2-20 μL pipette, transfer 12.5 µL WarmStart Master Mix into each reaction vessel.
Vortex mix all reagents for approximately 00:00:05 .
Using a 0.5-10 μL pipette, transfer 1 µL Target into the TARGET reaction vessel.
Vortex mix the reaction mixture.
Spin down reaction mixture.
Place reaction vessels into thermocycler.
Turn on thermocycler
Hit PROCEED to select a reaction cycle.
Scroll using the '<' and '>' keys to get to LAMP3.
Press PROCEED to begin
Spin down all reagents for approximately 00:00:05 .
creator: Kenneth Schackart
